overview: The TTX Filter processes Trados TTX documents, an XML-based bilingual format used by some versions of Trados tools and supported by several other translation tools. It extracts source and target text from segmented and unsegmented content, handles existing translations with match-type annotations, and preserves TTX structure on output. There are no official public specifications for the TTX format.

mode.segmentMode.help:
Controls how text is extracted based on the segmentation state of the TTX file.

- **Auto-detect** (default): The filter scans for at least one existing `<Tu>` segment. If found, behaves like "extract only existing"; if none found, behaves like "extract all".
- **Extract only existing segments**: Only text inside `<Tu>`/`<Tuv>` elements is extracted. If the file has no segments, **nothing is extracted**.
- **Extract all**: Both segmented and unsegmented text within `<Raw>` elements is extracted. Unsegmented text parts become separate segments alongside existing ones.
notes: Segmentation is often a cause for interoperability issues. For better compatibility with the tool that created the TTX files, it is recommended to work with **pre-segmented** documents.

output.escapeGT.help: Escapes all `>` characters as `>` in the output document. By default, only `<`, `&`, and quotes are escaped in XML output ã the greater-than sign is left unescaped since it is valid in XML text content. Enable this if downstream tools require strict escaping of `>`.

processingNotes: **Encoding detection**: Uses the document's XML encoding declaration if present; otherwise defaults to UTF-8 regardless of any encoding specified when opening the document.
**BOM handling**: When output is UTF-8 and input was also UTF-8, a BOM is written only if one was detected in the input. When converting from a non-UTF-8 input to UTF-8 output, no BOM is added.
**Line breaks**: Output preserves the same line-break style (CR, LF, or CRLF) as the original input.

**Existing translations**: Segmented entries with translations are extracted with both source and target. Match types are derived from the `MatchPercent` attribute: >100 with `Origin=xtranslate` ã `EXACT_LOCAL_CONTEXT` (XU matches in TagEditor), >99 ã `EXACT`, 1ã99 ã `FUZZY`. The `Origin` attribute value is carried over to the annotation.
TTX requires segmentation for target text representation ã the filter automatically generates `<Tu>` and `<Tuv>` elements as needed in the output.
limitations:
The TTX `` element may cause problems when it spans across external tag boundaries ã for example, when a `` for bold formatting crosses paragraph boundaries (`

`) that are external tags in TTX. These cases should be rare.

examples.title: Extract All mode ã segmented and unsegmented text
examples.description: In **Extract All** mode, text from both `<Raw>` content outside segments and inside existing `<Tu>` segments is extracted as a single text unit with multiple segments.

examples.title: Extract Existing Segments mode ã only segmented text
examples.description: In **Extract Only Existing Segments** mode, only the content inside `<Tu>`/`<Tuv>` elements is extracted. Unsegmented text outside those elements is ignored.
